REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF GLORIA YEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT FAT NOODLE 2ND SF, LLC IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING MITIGATION OF DAMAGES (SEE DEFENDANT'S MATERIAL FACTS 1 AND 2). ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE RE: CONTRACT. PROVIDING EVIDENCE WITH REPLY IS NOT PROPER. CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S ASSERTION CASTILLO V. GLENAIR (2018) 23 CAL. APPL.5TH 262 DOES NOT STAND FOR SUCH PROPOSITION. IN CASTILLO EVIDENCE WAS PRESENTED, BUT NOT REFERENCED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT; EVIDENCE WAS ALSO USED AND RELIED UPON BY THE OPPOSING PARTY. THIS IS NOT THE CASE HERE. FURTHER, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MAY ONLY BE GRANTED IF IT DISPOSES OF A CAUSE OF ACTION IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF HERE IS MOVING TO SUMMARILY ADJUDICATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, BUT DOES NOT ENTIRELY DISPOSE OF ONE OF THE ELEMENTS, I.E. DAMAGES TO BE PARTIALLY "PROVED AT A LATER DATE." (POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION 7:14 AND 7:17.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MR. PAVERS CONTRACTORS SERVICE, INC. NOTICE OF AND MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO C.C.P SECTION 877.6 IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY MET ALL TECH-BUILT FACTORS. OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS NOT WITHIN THE BALLPARK OF MR. PAVERS' LIABILITY. THE DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS MADE AT THE TIME OF THE SETTLEMENT. AT THAT TIME THERE WAS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY ALLEGED. EVEN IF SUCH CAUSE OF ACTION WERE ALLEGED, DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE VALUATION OF THE INDEMNITY WOULD HAVE CHANGED THE "BALLPARK," I.E. THERE IS NO AUTHORITY AND NO ARGUMENT PRESENTED SHOWING THAT MR. PAVERS, DUE TO INDEMNITY CLAUSE, WOULD HAVE TO INDEMNIFY THE DEFENDANTS FOR ALL DAMAGES CLAIMS BY THE PLAINTIFF, INCLUDING, FOR EXAMPLE, UNPAID RENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, INTERVENOR LEE WEINBERG, UNITED STATES LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS ALSO DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO BOTH NEGLIGENCE THEORIES ASSERTED AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT, IN FACT, SEEMINGLY CONCEDES THE EXISTENCE OF MANY FACTUAL DISPUTES REGARDING MATERIAL ISSUES. (SEE WEINBERG'S RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS, PP. 4-13.) ISSUES INCLUDE: " WHETHER DEFENDANT MADE ANY PERSONAL REPRESENTATIONS OR GUARANTEES REGARDING THE PERTINENT LEASE, AND IF SO, TO WHAT EXTENT. (PLAINTIFF'S NOL, EX. 2, P.95:8-25; EX.3, PP.67:21-68:7; DEFENDANT'S NOL, EX. 3, PP. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; WEINBERG DECL. 6.) " WHETHER, AND TO WHAT EXTENT, DID DEFENDANT ASSUME DIRECTION AND RESPONSIBILITY OF THE RENOVATION PROJECT, OR ANY PORTION THEREOF. (PLAINTIFF'S NOL, EX. 4, P.87:10-18; EX. 7; EX. 9; EX. 10; EX. 11; DEFENDANT'S NOL, EX. 3, PP. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; WEINBERG DECL. 9-10.) " WHETHER DEFENDANT AUTHORIZED ANY UNPERMITTED RENOVATION WORK, AND IF SO, WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED AS A KEY DECISION MAKER. (PLAINTIFF'S NOL, EX. 5, 79-80, 83:1-16, 147:15-25; EX. 7; EX. 11; PATTON DECL. 6-8; DEFENDANT'S NOL, EX. 3, PP. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; EX. 4, PP. 119:16-121:18; WEINBERG DECL. 8-12.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion By Lee Weinberg And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Notice Of Lodgment Of Exhibits 1 Through 21; Declarations Of Lee Weinberg And Ronald F. Berestka
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Seema Kadaba (925-838-2090) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Gloria Yee.
Ronald Berestke (925-400-9696) for Defendant/Moving party LEE WEINBERG, UNITED STATES LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY.

Hearing is held. Argued re: no lease agreement guarantee, two distinct issues, liability, no representations, and authority. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

INTERVENOR LEE WEINBERG, UNITED STATES LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion By Lee Weinberg And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Notice Of Lodgment Of Exhibits 1 Through 21; Declarations Of Lee Weinberg And Ronald F. Berestka

Motion for Summary Judgment is also DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. The Court finds triable issues of material fact as to both negligence theories asserted against Defendant. Defendant, in fact, seemingly concedes the existence of many factual disputes regarding material issues. (See Weinberg's Response to Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts, pp. 4-13.) Issues include:

" Whether Defendant made any personal representations or guarantees regarding the pertinent lease, and if so, to what extent. (Plaintiff's NOL, Ex. 2, p.95:8-25; Ex.3, pp.67:21-68:7; Defendant's NOL, Ex. 3, pp. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; Weinberg Decl. 6.)
" Whether, and to what extent, did Defendant assume direction and responsibility of the renovation project, or any portion thereof. (Plaintiff's NOL, Ex. 4, p.87:10-18; Ex. 7; Ex. 9; Ex. 10; Ex. 11; Defendant's NOL, Ex. 3, pp. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; Weinberg Decl. 9-10.)
" Whether Defendant authorized any unpermitted renovation work, and if so, whether Defendant acted as a key decision maker. (Plaintiff's NOL, Ex. 5, 79-80, 83:1-16, 147:15-25; Ex. 7; Ex. 11; Patton Decl. 6-8; Defendant's NOL, Ex. 3, pp. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; Ex. 4, pp. 119:16-121:18; Weinberg Decl. 8-12.)

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Notice Of And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To C.C.P Section 877.6
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT MR. PAVERS CONTRACTORS SERVICE, INC. Notice Of And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To C.C.P Section 877.6 is GRANTED. Moving party met all Tech-Built factors. Opposing party failed to show that the settlement is not within the ballpark of Mr. Pavers' liability. The determination of good faith settlement is made at the time of the settlement. At that time there was no cause of action for contractual indemnity alleged. Even if such cause of action were alleged, Defendants failed to show that the valuation of the indemnity would have changed the "ballpark," i.e. there is no authority and no argument presented showing that Mr. Pavers, due to indemnity clause, would have to indemnify the Defendants for all damages claims by the Plaintiff, including, for example, unpaid rent. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Breach Of Contract Cause Of Action Against Defendant Fat Noodle 2nd Sf, Llc
Off Calendar Jul-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GLORIA YEE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Breach Of Contract Cause Of Action Against Defendant Fat Noodle 2nd SF, LLC is DENIED. There is a triable issue of fact regarding mitigation of damages (See Defendant's Material Facts 1 and 2). Additionally, Plaintiff failed to shift its burden. Plaintiff failed to provide evidence re: contract. Providing evidence with reply is not proper. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertion Castillo v. Glenair (2018) 23 Cal. Appl.5th 262 does not stand for such proposition. In Castillo evidence was presented, but not referenced in the Separate Statement; evidence was also used and relied upon by the opposing party. This is not the case here. Further, summary adjudication may only be granted if it disposes of a cause of action in its entirety. Plaintiff here is moving to summarily adjudicate a cause of action for breach of contract, but does not entirely dispose of one of the elements, i.e. damages to be partially "proved at a later date." (Points and Authorities in Support of Motion 7:14 and 7:17.) Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
